channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: California sues Trump administration over executive order ending birthright citizenship
item.description: (FOX40.COM) — California Attorney General Rob Bonta announced his office would be joining 17 other states in suing the federal government over the newly inaugurated President Donald Trump's executive order ending birthright citizenship for those born in the country. The 14th Amendment to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jacque Porter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stefanik pledges an 'America First' agenda at the UN and a review of US funding
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rep. Elise Stefanik pledged Tuesday to push President Donald Trump’s “America First” stance if confirmed as U.S. ambassador to the United Nations and roll out a review of U.S. funding critical for U.N. operations and its many agencies around the world. Noting that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Trump wants to make showerheads and toilets flow greatly again, but so may utility bills
item.description: On his first day in office, President Donald Trump announced plans to pull America from the Paris Agreement and expand the production of oil, both campaign promises.
item.dc:creator: Alexa St. John And Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's first full day back in White House includes firings and an infrastructure announcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is spending his first full day back in the White House meeting with congressional leaders, announcing an investment in artificial intelligence infrastructure and demonstrating one of his favored expressions of power: firing people.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 22 states sue to stop Trump's order blocking birthright citizenship
item.description: Attorneys general from 22 states sued Tuesday to block President Donald Trump's move to end a century-old immigration practice known as birthright citizenship guaranteeing that U.S.-born children are citizens regardless of their parents’ status.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump cuts DEI regulations across federal government
item.description: In several executive orders signed Monday, Trump undid several Biden-era initiatives, saying they “end now.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota and other states sue to stop Trump's order blocking birthright citizenship
item.description: Attorneys general from 18 states including Minnesota sued to block President Donald Trump’s move to end a decades-old immigration policy known as birthright citizenship guaranteeing that U.S.-born children are citizens regardless of their parents’ status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's first day back in White House includes firings, infrastructure announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump is spending his first full day back in the White House meeting with congressional leaders, making an infrastructure announcement and demonstrating one of his favored expressions of power: firing people.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marco Rubio became secretary of state without a hitch. Keeping Trump's favor may be harder
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For Marco Rubio, the easy part is over. He was confirmed by the Senate as secretary of state with unanimous support, Democrats joining Republicans in praising his acumen and judgment.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At inaugural prayer service, bishop pleads for Trump to ‘have mercy’ on LGBTQ+ people and migrants
item.description: At the inaugural prayer service, the Right Rev. Mariann Budde, the Episcopal bishop of Washington, asked President Donald Trump to have mercy on the LGBTQ+ community and undocumented migrant workers.
item.dc:creator: Tiffany Stanley And Gary Fields, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump to announce newly formed partnership investing $500B in AI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday is announcing investments worth up to $500 billion for infrastructure tied to artificial intelligence by a new partnership formed by OpenAI, Oracle and SoftBank.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE faces early bumps with lawsuits and leadership shakeup
item.description: Lawsuits argue that DOGE should be considered an advisory committee which must adhere to transparency.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: 4 states sue to block illegal migrants from census count used to assign congressional seats, electoral votes
item.description: Louisiana, Ohio, Kansas and West Virginia sued to block illegal immigrants from being counted when apportioning congressional seats and electoral votes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chip Roy leads House Republicans in effort to repeal law used by Biden administration to prosecute pro-lifers
item.description: Rep. Chip Roy, R-Texas, has introduced legislation to repeal a law that was used by the Biden administration to prosecute pro-life activists who demonstrated outside abortion clinics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans look to probe some who received last-day Biden pardons
item.description: Congressional Republicans are looking to press on in scrutinizing some of those who received pardons from former President Biden in the last hours of his presidency. “Implication is that they needed the pardons… So, let’s call them all before Congress and demand the truth,” Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to make historic move towards revoking birthright citizenship
item.description: Trump wants to reinterpret the phrasing of the 14th Amendment to mean that the federal government would not recognize automatic birthright citizenship for children born in the U.S. to parents without legal status.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, PIX 11

item.title: Mayor Adams will attend Donald Trump’s inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams will attend President-elect Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday in Washington, D.C. Adams added the inauguration to his schedule Monday morning. He canceled existing appearances in New York City, including two celebrations of Martin Luther King Jr. Day.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Special session rebuttal on illegal immigration continues in Florida
item.description: The governor gave his order Monday with House and Senate leadership pushing back, and that's not the only rebuttal we've seen in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Court aims for fall trial date in sexual misconduct lawsuit against Fletcher
item.description: The lawsuit facing Nathan Fletcher over allegations of sexual misconduct could move to a civil trial as soon as fall 2025, as both sides continue to bump heads over text messages between the ex-supervisor's accuser and a friend.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Giuliani reaches tentative settlement with Georgia election workers
item.description: Attorneys for Rudy Giuliani and two former Georgia election workers said Thursday they have reached a tentative settlement, a remarkable twist that would end the workers’ extensive litigation to collect their $146 million defamation judgment.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Local reactions mixed as Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal moves forward
item.description: The Middle East and the world are celebrating the Israel-Hamas ceasefire as a possible step toward peace, but many are still cautious about the deal.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada attorney general moves to protect health care access for DACA recipients
item.description: Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford is intervening to protect access to health care insurance for Dreamers — the recipients of the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) policy.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
